message:
perf(string): fast-path strcspn empty and single-byte reject sets (bd-os0um)

Specialize the safe strcspn core scan for the two reject-set sizes that
dominate real callers — empty (degenerates to strlen) and single byte
(common when callers ask "find first occurrence of a delimiter that
also stops at NUL"). Both shapes can skip the per-character membership
test loop entirely.

- reject_len == 0: return strlen(s) directly. Without this, the generic
  loop still walks every byte and tests an empty reject_set, which is
  wasted work for a no-op call.
- reject_len == 1: inline the single-byte equality check alongside the
  NUL-terminator check in one tight loop, avoiding the slice-membership
  branch on the generic path.
- Preserves existing NUL-termination semantics: the scan still stops at
  the first NUL in s, matching POSIX strcspn behavior.

Adds test_strcspn_single_reject_match_without_terminator covering the
new single-byte fast path with a non-NUL match before any terminator.

Beads bd-os0um (Optimize core strcspn single-reject scan with benchmark
proof) tracks the parity/perf slice; status flipped to in_progress
since this lands the implementation portion ahead of the benchmark
capture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1041,6 +1041,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-olgi","title":"Track B: Implement dlinfo for RTLD_DI_LINKMAP and RTLD_DI_ORIGIN","description":"## Goal
Replace the dlinfo stub in glibc_internal_abi.rs (line 4290) that returns ENOSYS with a real implementation supporting the two most commonly used request codes.

## Current State
```rust
pub unsafe extern "C" fn dlinfo(_handle: *mut c_void, _request: c_int, _info: *mut c_void) -> c_int {
    unsafe { *libc::__errno_location() = libc::ENOSYS };
    -1
}
```

## Why This Matters
Programs using dlinfo include:
- Python (ctypes.util.find_library -> RTLD_DI_LINKMAP to walk loaded libs)
- Ruby FFI, JVM, Lua FFI for plugin/library discovery
- Debugging tools (ltrace, strace analysis scripts)
- Programs checking RTLD_DI_ORIGIN to find their own install directory

## Supported Request Codes

### RTLD_DI_LINKMAP (2) — Write struct link_map* to info
The struct link_map (defined in <link.h>) has this layout:
```c
struct link_map {
    ElfW(Addr) l_addr;    // Base address of shared object
    char      *l_name;    // Absolute file name of shared object
    ElfW(Dyn) *l_ld;      // Dynamic section pointer
    struct link_map *l_next, *l_prev;  // Chain of loaded objects
};
```

Implementation:
- For RTLD_DEFAULT / NULL handle: return a static link_map for libfrankenlibc_abi.so itself. Populate l_addr and l_name from /proc/self/maps by finding our own library. l_ld can be null. l_next/l_prev = null (single entry).
- For other handles: delegate to host dlinfo via resolve_host_symbol_raw('dlinfo') if available. This is the interpose model — host-loaded libraries are the host linker's responsibility.
- If host delegation unavailable: return -1 with errno=ENOENT.

### RTLD_DI_ORIGIN (6) — Write directory path string to info (char* buffer)
- Parse /proc/self/maps to find the pathname for the handle's load address range.
- Extract dirname (everything up to last '/').
- Copy into info buffer (caller must provide sufficient space — POSIX says PATH_MAX).
- For RTLD_DEFAULT: return dirname of libfrankenlibc_abi.so.
- For unknown handles: delegate to host or return -1 with EINVAL.

### Other request codes: Return -1 with errno=ENOSYS
RTLD_DI_SERINFO (4), RTLD_DI_SERINFOSIZE (5), RTLD_DI_TLS_MODID (9), RTLD_DI_TLS_DATA (10) — rare, remain ENOSYS.

## Design Details

### Self-identification
We already have /proc/self/maps parsing in host_resolve.rs (loaded_libc_base_and_path). Reuse that to find our own library path. Cache the result in a static OnceLock<(u64, String)>.

### Handle classification
- NULL or RTLD_DEFAULT (pseudo-handle): self
- Address within our .so's mapped range: self
- Otherwise: delegate to host dlinfo

### Static link_map for self
Allocate a static link_map struct (leaked Box) on first call. Populate with:
- l_addr = our base address from /proc/self/maps
- l_name = leaked CString of our library path
- l_ld = null (we don't expose our own dynamic section)
- l_next = l_prev = null

## Files Changed
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs: Replace dlinfo body (~60 lines)
- May add a helper fn in host_resolve.rs for self-identification

## Unit Tests (in tests/glibc_internal_abi_test.rs or tests/dlfcn_abi_test.rs)
- test dlinfo_origin_default_returns_valid_directory: dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) writes a non-empty path that is a real directory (stat it)
- test dlinfo_origin_null_handle_returns_directory: dlinfo(NULL, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) same as above
- test dlinfo_linkmap_default_returns_nonnull: dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_LINKMAP, &ptr) writes a non-null link_map* with valid l_name
- test dlinfo_linkmap_l_name_is_valid_path: the l_name in the returned link_map is a readable file path
- test dlinfo_unsupported_request_returns_enosys: dlinfo(NULL, 999, ptr) returns -1, errno=ENOSYS
- test dlinfo_bad_handle_returns_error: dlinfo(0xDEADBEEF, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) returns -1

## e2e Test Script: scripts/check_dlinfo_native.sh
Build and run a C test program under LD_PRELOAD that:
1. Calls dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) and prints origin
2. Calls dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_LINKMAP, &lm) and prints l_name
3. Verifies both return 0
4. Emits structured JSONL report

## Structured Logging
dlinfo emits: trace_id, api_family=dlfcn, symbol=dlinfo, request_code, handle_class (self|host|unknown), result, errno, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
- dlinfo handles RTLD_DI_LINKMAP and RTLD_DI_ORIGIN for default/self handles
- Host delegation for non-self handles when host dlinfo available
- ENOSYS for unsupported request codes, EINVAL for bad handles
- 6+ unit tests pass
- e2e script passes under LD_PRELOAD
- cargo clippy clean
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite not regressed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:50.994367570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:48:34.754188981Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:48:34.753906196Z","close_reason":"Implemented dlinfo for RTLD_DI_LINKMAP and RTLD_DI_ORIGIN. Self-identification via /proc/self/maps (cached in OnceLock). RTLD_DI_LINKMAP: returns static leaked link_map with base addr and pathname for default/self handle, delegates to host for other handles. RTLD_DI_ORIGIN: extracts dirname from library path. Unsupported requests return ENOSYS. Clippy clean, LD_PRELOAD smoke passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dlfcn","native","stub-elimination"],"comments":[{"id":1362,"issue_id":"bd-olgi","author":"ubuntu","text":"dlinfo is a GNU extension (not POSIX). Key use cases: (1) RTLD_DI_LINKMAP - Python ctypes, Ruby FFI, JVM enumerate loaded libs. For interpose model, delegate to host dlinfo for host handles, return minimal struct for our own. (2) RTLD_DI_ORIGIN - directory of shared object from /proc/self/maps. RTLD_DI_SERINFO/SERINFOSIZE/TLS_MODID are rare, can remain ENOSYS.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-oraci","title":"dladdr returns 0 for valid function pointer","description":"execute_dladdr_case reports impl_output=0 (failure) for a valid function pointer where host glibc returns nonzero (success). Fixture loader_edges.json case dladdr_valid_address_strict expects 'nonzero' matching host. Real implementation gap in frankenlibc_abi::dlfcn_abi::dladdr (or the fixture executor's call shape). Exposed while adding harness-matrix coverage under bd-k2d8; the in-process + subprocess tests skip this specific case until the gap is fixed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:40:39.073201432Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:14:56.965372269Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:14:56.963016731Z","close_reason":"host_dladdr_cached now lazily bootstraps HOST_DLADDR via load_host_symbol; loader_edges_conformance_test::dladdr_valid_address_strict now passes without KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS skip. Committed 8cfba52 and pushed to main+master.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-os0u","title":"syscall: sched_getaffinity cpu_set veneer","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T09:25:58.430468203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T09:30:23.915577769Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T09:30:23.913032384Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-os0um","title":"Optimize core strcspn single-reject scan with benchmark proof","description":"Spec-first parity/performance slice for POSIX strcspn: capture the existing strcspn_absent Criterion baseline, specialize the safe core single-byte reject path without changing NUL-terminated semantics, and verify core/ABI strcspn tests plus fmt/check/clippy/UBS and before/after benchmark evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:15:12.347289308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:15:47.382867813Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","performance","string"]}
 {"id":"bd-osb8","title":"Track C: Implement gshadow parsing for getsgent/getsgnam/fgetsgent/sgetsgent","description":"## Goal
Replace 8 gshadow stub functions in pwd_abi.rs (lines 969-1040) with real implementations that parse /etc/gshadow.

## Current State
All 8 functions (4 base + 4 _r reentrant) return null or ENOENT without reading /etc/gshadow:
- getsgent() -> null (should enumerate gshadow entries sequentially)
- getsgnam(name) -> null (should look up by group name)
- fgetsgent(stream) -> null (should parse one entry from a FILE*)
- sgetsgent(string) -> null (should parse one entry from a string)
Plus _r reentrant variants that take caller-supplied buffers.

## Why This Matters
Programs that manipulate group shadow passwords (gpasswd, newgrp, certain PAM modules) call these functions. While rare in most workloads, returning null silently breaks shadow group authentication. The fix is straightforward since we already have parsers for /etc/passwd and /etc/group.

## gshadow File Format
Each line: group_name:encrypted_password:administrators:members
Fields separated by colons. Example:
  root:*::
  shadow:*::
  sudo:!::user1,user2

## Design
Two-layer approach matching existing pwd/grp pattern:

### Layer 1: Core parser (frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/ or new gshadow module)
- parse_gshadow_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<GshadowEntry>
- GshadowEntry { name, password, admins: Vec, members: Vec }
- lookup_gshadow_by_name(content: &[u8], name: &[u8]) -> Option<GshadowEntry>
- Reuse the same colon-delimited parsing pattern as parse_passwd_line/parse_group_line

### Layer 2: ABI wiring (pwd_abi.rs)
- Static sgrp struct (matching glibc struct sgrp layout) for non-_r functions
- Thread-local enumeration state for getsgent/setsgent/endsgent
- Read /etc/gshadow via SYS_openat/SYS_read (no libc dependency)
- The _r variants write into caller-supplied buffers

## Testing
- Unit test: parse valid gshadow line with all fields
- Unit test: parse line with empty password, no admins, no members
- Unit test: getsgnam returns correct entry for known group
- Unit test: getsgnam returns null for nonexistent group
- Unit test: getsgent enumerates all entries when called repeatedly
- Unit test: sgetsgent parses a single line correctly
- Unit test: _r variants return ERANGE when buffer is too small

## Acceptance Criteria
All 8 gshadow functions parse /etc/gshadow correctly. Unit tests pass for positive, negative, and edge cases. Clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:17.296873153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:53:04.143326112Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:53:04.142959658Z","close_reason":"Both children closed: C.1 (gshadow parser, 14 tests) and C.2 (ABI wiring, 34 pwd tests). All 8 gshadow functions implemented: getsgent/getsgnam/sgetsgent parse /etc/gshadow, getsgent_r/getsgnam_r/sgetsgent_r use caller buffers. fgetsgent/fgetsgent_r return ENOENT (safe for interpose model). setsgent/endsgent manage enumeration state.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["native","pwd","stub-elimination"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-osb8","depends_on_id":"bd-kcbm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:02.662018084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-osb8","depends_on_id":"bd-ta3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:02.519365590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1363,"issue_id":"bd-osb8","author":"ubuntu","text":"Priority rationale: gshadow used by gpasswd/newgrp/grpck only. Most systems lack /etc/gshadow. Fix is < 200 lines since we already parse /etc/passwd, /etc/group, /etc/shadow with identical colon-delimited format. Format: name:password:admins:members.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-otvz","title":"Execute strlen_strict fixture through isolated harness","description":"Upgrade strlen_strict conformance from schema-only validation to per-case isolated harness execution, plus top-level fixture metadata assertions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-17T00:39:55.354416631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T00:44:29.254212509Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T00:44:29.251492016Z","close_reason":"Upgraded strlen_strict conformance coverage to execute every fixture case through the isolated harness subprocess path and added top-level fixture metadata assertions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ovcrt","title":"PORT: setmode + getmode (BSD chmod symbolic-mode parser/applier)","description":"BSD setmode(mode_str) parses a chmod-style symbolic mode string ("u+x", "g-w", "a=rwx", "u=rwx,go=rx") into an opaque "bitbox" of operations. Companion getmode(bbox, current_mode) applies the operations to current_mode and returns the new mode. Used by chmod(1), find(1) -perm, install(1), mtree, and many other BSD utilities. The full POSIX 1003.1 grammar covers comma-chained clauses, who-spec (u/g/o/a/empty), op (+/-/=), and perm (rwx + s/t/X + u/g/o-as-perm). For v1 ship the common subset: comma-chained clauses, u/g/o/a/empty who, +/-/= op, r/w/x/s/t perms (skip X conditional and u/g/o-as-perm copy semantics — both are uncommon in real config files). Ship: pure-safe core::stdlib::setmode::{parse, apply, ChmodOp} + thin abi shims that allocate the opaque bitbox via malloc.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:29:19.325396211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:37:18.646132252Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:37:18.643708885Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::stdlib::setmode (parse + apply) + stdlib_abi setmode/getmode with malloc'd magic-header bitbox; 23+11 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
